sections.paragraph:
ἀνακτάομαι fut. ἀνακτήσομαι TestJob 40:4; 1 aor. ἀνεκτησάμην (Trag., Hdt. et al.; ins, pap; Sym. 1 Km 30:12 al.; TestJob 44:4) w. ἑαυτόν regain one’s strength, renew one’s energy (Epict. 3, 25, 4; PFay 106, 18 ὅπως δυνηθῶ ἐμαυτὸν ἀνακτήσασθαι; Jos., Ant. 9, 123; 15, 365; OdeSol 11:11). ἀνακτήσασθε (ἑαυτοὺς ἐν πίστει) ITr 8:1 is J-BCotelier’s conjecture for the ἀνακτίσασθε of the mss.—DELG s.v. κτάομαι.

fut. fut. = fut.

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

aor. aor. = aorist

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 66
